title: campanula medium var. bourdiniana
intro: Campanula medium var. bourdiniana, also known as Canterbury bells, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the bellflower family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and act as an expectorant to ease congestion. The health benefits of Campanula medium var. bourdiniana include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help soothe sore throats, coughs, and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although it's essential to consult with a medical professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
